pattern: Because removing a two-letter pattern only ever depends on which letters are currently adjacent, greedily clearing the higher-scoring pattern first everywhere it can appear (with a stack), then clearing whatever is left of the lower-scoring pattern, always beats interleaving the two removal types.
hints: Whichever of "ab" or "ba" is worth more points should always be removed first, everywhere it occurs, before touching the other pattern.

You can strip every occurrence of a single two-letter pattern from a string in one left-to-right pass with a stack: push characters, and whenever the top of the stack plus the current character form the pattern, pop instead of pushing and add its score.
After exhausting the higher-value pattern this way, run the exact same stack pass over what remains to remove the lower-value pattern, and sum both scores.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, ba worth more than ab
statement example, ab worth more than ba
only the lower-priority pattern is present
third unrelated letter breaks up otherwise removable runs

stress.description: random string over a/b/c so both patterns and blockers are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 400))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('aabbc') for _ in range(n))
    x = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    y = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    return [s, x, y]
stress.note: kept modest so the pass-based oracle stays fast
